inspiration_source: Listening to natural EM fields of the Earth
physical_translation: Natural EM fields (lightning, solar wind) induce telluric currents; conductivity contrasts from ore bodies alter the measured response
recommended_action: Identify any open MT datasets near validated zones (Kalgoorlie, Chuquicamata)

inspiration_source: The Earth constantly vibrates — passive listening
physical_translation: Microtremors from ocean waves and wind interact with subsurface structure; H/V spectral ratio reveals impedance contrasts
recommended_action: Check IRIS station coverage near Kalgoorlie and Chuquicamata

inspiration_source: Subsurface conduction paths mirror ore deposit geometry
physical_translation: Sulfide mineralization increases bulk conductivity; alteration zones change resistivity — detectable with AEM or EM surveys
recommended_action: Monitor GA/GSWA for open AEM data releases

inspiration_source: Rock types cool differently after sunset — ore bodies stand out
physical_translation: Thermal inertia varies by rock type; metallic minerals conduct heat faster, leading to measurable night-time temperature differences
recommended_action: Build day-night thermal difference features from Landsat pairs at one pilot zone

inspiration_source: Altered ground dries at a different rate — visible from orbit
physical_translation: Altered/mineralized rock has different porosity and permeability, causing differential drying rates detectable in time-series reflectance
recommended_action: Build differential reflectance feature (post-rain minus pre-rain) at one pilot zone

inspiration_source: Dense ore bodies pull — even gravity maps know where they are
physical_translation: Dense ore bodies produce measurable gravity anomalies; Bouguer anomaly maps reveal subsurface mass distribution
recommended_action: Test WGM2012 as coarse regional proxy while awaiting GA GADDS access

inspiration_source: The ground moves slowly over geological structures
physical_translation: Subsidence, uplift, or slow ground movement associated with mining or geological processes is detectable via SAR coherence loss or InSAR deformation maps
recommended_action: Build SAR coherence features at one pilot zone; assess correlation with known structural targets

hypothesis_name: Natural EM Signals of the Earth (Schumann Resonances)
inspiration_source: The planet resonates at its own frequencies
physical_translation: Earth's EM cavity resonates at ~7.83 Hz and harmonics; local conductivity variations theoretically modulate these global signals
recommended_action: Literature review only — do not attempt practical testing without specialist equipment and peer-reviewed protocol

inspiration_source: Natural electromagnetic signals might concentrate over mineral targets
physical_translation: Time-varying magnetic field measurements near known deposits show anomalous spectral content compared to background readings
recommended_action: Literature search for published MT-mineralization correlation studies before committing resources

inspiration_source: Some areas hold heat differently after sunset
physical_translation: Multi-temporal thermal imaging reveals locations that cool slower or faster than surroundings — a proxy for subsurface thermal properties
recommended_action: Select one pilot zone; build cooling-rate anomaly index from day/night Landsat thermal band pairs

inspiration_source: The ground responds differently to seasons over different geology
physical_translation: Amplitude and phase of seasonal vegetation/moisture cycles differ over mineralized ground vs barren background
recommended_action: Build seasonal amplitude and phase features from multi-year NDVI and LST; test at Kalgoorlie pilot

inspiration_source: Combined passive signals reveal patterns invisible to any single sensor
physical_translation: Feature stacking from multiple passive sources (thermal + SAR + spectral + temporal) with ML classification uncovers compound signatures
recommended_action: Systematic multi-sensor passive-only fusion experiment at best-performing zone; compare to existing fusion results

hypothesis_name: Differential Terrain Response to Rain/Drought/Heat Episodes
inspiration_source: The land remembers extreme events differently over different geology
physical_translation: NDVI and soil moisture recovery rates after drought or extreme heat events differ over mineralized vs background ground
recommended_action: Identify historical extreme weather events, compare NDVI/moisture recovery rates at deposits vs background

hypothesis_name: Local Time-Series Anomaly as Resonance Proxy
inspiration_source: Resonance of the terrain — certain frequencies repeat over certain geology
physical_translation: Spectral (Fourier) analysis of multi-year NDVI or thermal time series reveals anomalous periodicity or amplitude at mineralized sites
recommended_action: Fourier analysis of long NDVI time series at known deposit sites vs background; apply strict confounder controls

inspiration_source: The ground remembers past geological events
physical_translation: No direct physical equivalent — geological history is encoded in current measurable properties (conductivity, density, magnetism), not in a retrievable 'memory'
recommended_action: Reframe as specific measurable hypotheses (thermal persistence, cooling rate) before attempting any experiment

physical_translation: No measurement protocol exists for subsurface intent or consciousness — the useful residue is that subsurface properties modulate surface observables, which is standard remote sensing
recommended_action: Extract the physical residue (surface modulation by subsurface structure) and pursue that — not the original framing

inspiration_source: Traditional practice of detecting subsurface features with rods or pendulums
physical_translation: No reproducible controlled experiment has demonstrated above-chance performance for the literal mechanism — the operator's geological knowledge and pattern recognition are the real signal
recommended_action: The value is in the operator's geological knowledge — encode that knowledge as interpretable features, not the claimed dowsing mechanism

inspiration_source: Specific frequencies of the Earth could indicate minerals
physical_translation: While Schumann resonances exist, local modulation by mineral deposits has never been demonstrated at exploration scale in peer-reviewed literature
recommended_action: Monitor literature; status upgrades to SPECULATIVE_BUT_TESTABLE only if peer-reviewed evidence of local EM anomaly-deposit correlation is published
